services.description: Long-running workloads deployed as Kubernetes Deployments with a ClusterIP Service. Each service gets a CiliumNetworkPolicy for traffic control. Pods are spread across nodes via topology constraints.

name.description: Unique name for the service. Used as the Deployment name, Service name, pod label (platform.lwsa.tech/service), and in network policy selectors.

plan.description: Resource plan that determines CPU and memory requests/limits for the container. Plans are predefined tiers from pico (smallest) to 4xlarge (largest).

port.description: Container port the application listens on. Defaults to 10000. Also injected as the PORT environment variable.
Additional container port number. Each additional port can optionally be exposed via its own LoadBalancer Service.

expose.description: Exposes this additional port via a dedicated LoadBalancer Service with optional IP allowlisting.

enabled.description: When true, creates a LoadBalancer Service for this additional port, making it reachable from outside the cluster.

sourceRanges.description: CIDR ranges allowed to reach this port through the LoadBalancer. If omitted, all source IPs are allowed.

additionalPorts.description: Additional container ports beyond the primary port. Each gets its own CiliumNetworkPolicy ingress rule, and can optionally be exposed via LoadBalancer.

replicas.description: Number of pod replicas. Defaults to 1. Cannot exceed 1 when mountVolumes is used, because volumes are ReadWriteOnce (RWO).

autoscaling.description: Configures horizontal pod autoscaling via KEDA ScaledObject. When enabled, KEDA manages replica count between minReplicas and maxReplicas based on the configured triggers.

enabled.description: When true, creates a KEDA ScaledObject that automatically scales the service based on the configured triggers.

minReplicas.description: Minimum number of replicas KEDA will scale down to. Set to 0 to allow scale-to-zero. Defaults to 1.

maxReplicas.description: Maximum number of replicas KEDA will scale up to. Defaults to 10.

triggers.description: KEDA trigger specifications that determine when and how to scale. If omitted, defaults to a CPU trigger at 200% average utilization.

items.description: A KEDA trigger definition. Supports any KEDA-compatible scaler type (cpu, memory, prometheus, rabbitmq, etc.).

authenticationRef.description: Reference to a TriggerAuthentication or ClusterTriggerAuthentication resource that provides credentials for the scaler.

kind.description: Kind of the authentication resource. Defaults to TriggerAuthentication if omitted.

name.description: Name of the TriggerAuthentication or ClusterTriggerAuthentication resource.
Optional name for this trigger, used to identify it in metrics and logs.

metricType.description: How the metric target is interpreted: 'Value' (absolute), 'AverageValue' (per-pod average), or 'Utilization' (percentage of resource request).

useCachedMetrics.description: When true, KEDA caches the metric value between polling intervals instead of querying on every evaluation.

metadata.description: Scaler-specific configuration parameters. Contents depend on the trigger type (e.g., for cpu: {type: 'Utilization', value: '200'}).

command.description: Overrides the container entrypoint (ENTRYPOINT). Each element is a separate argument in the exec form.

args.description: Arguments passed to the container entrypoint (CMD). Used alongside or instead of command.

ingress.description: Creates a Kubernetes Ingress resource to expose the service via HTTPS through the ingress-nginx controller. Automatically provisions TLS certificates for configured hosts.

enabled.description: When true, creates an Ingress resource that routes external HTTPS traffic to this service's primary port.

hosts.description: Custom hostnames (e.g., 'app.example.com') for TLS certificate provisioning. The cluster's auto-generated domain is always included in addition to these.

sourceRanges.description: CIDR ranges allowed to access the Ingress. If omitted, all source IPs are allowed.

autoInstrumentation.description: Enables OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation by adding OTel annotations to the pod spec, allowing the OTel operator to inject tracing/metrics sidecars.

enabled.description: When true, adds OpenTelemetry annotations to the pod, enabling automatic trace and metrics collection.

expose.description: Exposes the service's primary port via a LoadBalancer Service, making it reachable from outside the cluster on a cloud-assigned IP.

enabled.description: When true, creates a LoadBalancer Service for the primary container port and adds corresponding CiliumNetworkPolicy ingress rules.

sourceRanges.description: CIDR ranges allowed to reach the LoadBalancer. If omitted, all source IPs are allowed.

mountVolumes.description: Mounts persistent volumes (defined in the top-level 'volumes' array) into the container. Switching the Deployment strategy to Recreate (required for RWO volumes). An init container is created when ownership is specified.

name.description: Name of the volume to mount. Must match a volume defined in the top-level 'volumes' array.

path.description: Absolute path inside the container where the volume will be mounted (e.g., '/data').

ownership.description: Sets ownership of the mount point via an init container that runs chown before the main container starts. Format: 'user:group' (numeric or named, e.g., '1000:1000' or 'www-data:www-data').

mountConfigMaps.description: Mounts ConfigMaps (defined in the top-level 'configmaps' array) as files inside the container.

name.description: Name of the ConfigMap to mount. Must match a ConfigMap defined in the top-level 'configmaps' array.

path.description: Absolute path inside the container where the ConfigMap will be mounted as a directory of files.

envVars.description: Static environment variables injected into the container. Each key becomes an env var name, each value becomes the env var value.

envVarsFrom.description: Injects environment variables from external sources: Kubernetes Secrets, ConfigMaps, database servers, search engines, or message brokers. Each source type auto-generates connection-related env vars.

secrets.description: Injects env vars from Kubernetes Secrets. By default all keys from the Secret are injected; use mappings to select specific keys or rename them.

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret to source env vars from.

mappings.description: Selective key injection. Keys are the env var names to set, values are the Secret keys to read from. If omitted, all Secret keys are injected as env vars.

configMaps.description: Injects env vars from Kubernetes ConfigMaps. By default all keys are injected; use mappings to select specific keys or rename them.

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes ConfigMap to source env vars from.

mappings.description: Selective key injection. Keys are the env var names to set, values are the ConfigMap keys to read from. If omitted, all ConfigMap keys are injected as env vars.

dbServers.description: Auto-injects database connection env vars from a database server defined in the top-level 'dbServers' array. The injected vars depend on the database type: pxc/cnpg inject DB_HOST, DB_HOST_READ, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_NAME, DATABASE_URL; dragonfly injects REDIS_HOST, REDIS_PASSWORD, REDIS_URL, MEMCACHED_HOST; psmdb/ferretdb inject MONGODB_HOST, MONGODB_USERNAME, MONGODB_PASSWORD, MONGODB_DATABASE, MONGODB_URI.

name.description: Name of the database server (must match a dbServers[].name entry).

type.description: Database type. Must match the type of the referenced dbServer. Determines which connection env vars are injected.

mappings.description: Override auto-generated env var names. Keys are the custom env var names to set, values are the default env var names to map from (e.g., {'MY_DB_HOST': 'DB_HOST'}).

searches.description: Auto-injects Elasticsearch connection env vars: ELASTICSEARCH_HOST, ELASTICSEARCH_PORT, ELASTICSEARCH_USER, ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD, ELASTICSEARCH_URL (https scheme).

name.description: Name of the search engine (must match a searches[].name entry).

type.description: Search engine type. Currently only 'elasticsearch' is supported.

mappings.description: Override auto-generated env var names. Keys are the custom env var names, values are the default env var names to map from.

messageBrokers.description: Auto-injects message broker connection env vars. For RabbitMQ: RABBITMQ_HOST, RABBITMQ_PORT, RABBITMQ_USER, RABBITMQ_PASSWORD, RABBITMQ_URL.

name.description: Name of the message broker (must match a messageBrokers[].name entry).

type.description: Broker type. Currently only 'rabbitmq' is supported.

mappings.description: Override auto-generated env var names. Keys are the custom env var names, values are the default env var names to map from.

allowFrom.description: Defines CiliumNetworkPolicy ingress rules allowing traffic from specific services or cronjobs in other namespaces to reach this service.

services.description: Services from other namespaces allowed to send traffic to this service. Creates CiliumNetworkPolicy ingress rules matching on the source pod's platform.lwsa.tech/service label.

name.description: Name of the source service (matches platform.lwsa.tech/service label).

cronjobs.description: CronJobs from other namespaces allowed to send traffic to this service. Creates CiliumNetworkPolicy ingress rules matching on the source pod's platform.lwsa.tech/cronjob label.

name.description: Name of the source cronjob (matches platform.lwsa.tech/cronjob label).

allowTo.description: Defines CiliumNetworkPolicy egress rules allowing this service to send traffic to specific services in other namespaces.

services.description: Services in other namespaces this service is allowed to send traffic to. Creates CiliumNetworkPolicy egress rules matching on the destination pod's platform.lwsa.tech/service label.

name.description: Name of the destination service (matches platform.lwsa.tech/service label).

egress.description: Controls outbound traffic via CiliumNetworkPolicy egress rules. DNS, S3, MinIO, and intra-namespace traffic are always allowed regardless of these settings.

enabled.description: When false, blocks all egress traffic except DNS, S3, MinIO, and intra-namespace. Defaults to true (all egress allowed).

toCIDRs.description: IP ranges this service is allowed to reach. Added as CiliumNetworkPolicy egress CIDR rules. Only effective when egress.enabled is true.

port.description: Optional port restriction. If omitted, all ports to this CIDR are allowed.

protocol.description: Protocol for the port restriction. Defaults to TCP if port is specified.

toFQDNs.description: Domain names this service is allowed to reach. Added as CiliumNetworkPolicy FQDN-based egress rules. Only effective when egress.enabled is true.

pattern.description: Wildcard domain pattern to allow (e.g., '*.example.com'). Mutually exclusive with 'host'.

host.description: Exact domain name to allow (e.g., 'api.example.com'). Mutually exclusive with 'pattern'.

port.description: Optional port restriction. If omitted, all ports to this FQDN are allowed.

protocol.description: Protocol for the port restriction. Defaults to TCP if port is specified.

cronjobs.description: Scheduled workloads deployed as Kubernetes CronJobs. Each cronjob gets a CiliumNetworkPolicy for traffic control. Pods use restartPolicy: OnFailure with a 3-day active deadline.

name.description: Unique name for the cronjob. Used as the CronJob name, pod label (platform.lwsa.tech/cronjob), and in network policy selectors.

plan.description: Resource plan that determines CPU and memory requests/limits for the container.

command.description: Overrides the container entrypoint (ENTRYPOINT). Each element is a separate argument in the exec form.

args.description: Arguments passed to the container entrypoint (CMD). Used alongside or instead of command.

mountVolumes.description: Mounts persistent volumes (defined in the top-level 'volumes' array) into the container.

name.description: Name of the volume to mount. Must match a volume defined in the top-level 'volumes' array.

path.description: Absolute path inside the container where the volume will be mounted.

ownership.description: Sets ownership of the mount point via an init container that runs chown before the main container starts. Format: 'user:group' (numeric or named, e.g., '1000:1000').

mountConfigMaps.description: Mounts ConfigMaps (defined in the top-level 'configmaps' array) as files inside the container.

name.description: Name of the ConfigMap to mount. Must match a ConfigMap defined in the top-level 'configmaps' array.

path.description: Absolute path inside the container where the ConfigMap will be mounted as a directory of files.

envVars.description: Static environment variables injected into the container. Each key becomes an env var name, each value becomes the env var value.

envVarsFrom.description: Injects environment variables from external sources: Kubernetes Secrets, ConfigMaps, database servers, search engines, or message brokers.

secrets.description: Injects env vars from Kubernetes Secrets. By default all keys from the Secret are injected; use mappings to select specific keys or rename them.

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret to source env vars from.

mappings.description: Selective key injection. Keys are the env var names to set, values are the Secret keys to read from. If omitted, all Secret keys are injected as env vars.

configMaps.description: Injects env vars from Kubernetes ConfigMaps. By default all keys are injected; use mappings to select specific keys or rename them.

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes ConfigMap to source env vars from.

mappings.description: Selective key injection. Keys are the env var names to set, values are the ConfigMap keys to read from. If omitted, all ConfigMap keys are injected as env vars.

dbServers.description: Auto-injects database connection env vars from a database server defined in the top-level 'dbServers' array. The injected vars depend on the database type: pxc/cnpg inject DB_HOST, DB_HOST_READ, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_NAME, DATABASE_URL; dragonfly injects REDIS_HOST, REDIS_PASSWORD, REDIS_URL, MEMCACHED_HOST; psmdb/ferretdb inject MONGODB_HOST, MONGODB_USERNAME, MONGODB_PASSWORD, MONGODB_DATABASE, MONGODB_URI.

name.description: Name of the database server (must match a dbServers[].name entry).

type.description: Database type. Must match the type of the referenced dbServer. Determines which connection env vars are injected.

mappings.description: Override auto-generated env var names. Keys are the custom env var names to set, values are the default env var names to map from.

searches.description: Auto-injects Elasticsearch connection env vars: ELASTICSEARCH_HOST, ELASTICSEARCH_PORT, ELASTICSEARCH_USER, ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD, ELASTICSEARCH_URL.

name.description: Name of the search engine (must match a searches[].name entry).

type.description: Search engine type. Currently only 'elasticsearch' is supported.

mappings.description: Override auto-generated env var names. Keys are the custom env var names, values are the default env var names to map from.

messageBrokers.description: Auto-injects message broker connection env vars. For RabbitMQ: RABBITMQ_HOST, RABBITMQ_PORT, RABBITMQ_USER, RABBITMQ_PASSWORD, RABBITMQ_URL.

name.description: Name of the message broker (must match a messageBrokers[].name entry).

type.description: Broker type. Currently only 'rabbitmq' is supported.

mappings.description: Override auto-generated env var names. Keys are the custom env var names, values are the default env var names to map from.

allowTo.description: Defines CiliumNetworkPolicy egress rules allowing this cronjob to send traffic to specific services in other namespaces.

services.description: Services in other namespaces this cronjob is allowed to send traffic to.

name.description: Name of the destination service (matches platform.lwsa.tech/service label).

egress.description: Controls outbound traffic via CiliumNetworkPolicy egress rules. DNS, S3, MinIO, and intra-namespace traffic are always allowed regardless of these settings.

enabled.description: When false, blocks all egress traffic except DNS, S3, MinIO, and intra-namespace. Defaults to true (all egress allowed).

toCIDRs.description: IP ranges this cronjob is allowed to reach.

port.description: Optional port restriction. If omitted, all ports to this CIDR are allowed.

protocol.description: Protocol for the port restriction. Defaults to TCP if port is specified.

toFQDNs.description: Domain names this cronjob is allowed to reach.

pattern.description: Wildcard domain pattern to allow (e.g., '*.example.com'). Mutually exclusive with 'host'.

host.description: Exact domain name to allow (e.g., 'api.example.com'). Mutually exclusive with 'pattern'.

port.description: Optional port restriction. If omitted, all ports to this FQDN are allowed.

protocol.description: Protocol for the port restriction. Defaults to TCP if port is specified.

schedule.description: Cron schedule expression (e.g., '*/5 * * * *' for every 5 minutes, '0 2 * * *' for daily at 2 AM). Uses standard 5-field cron syntax.

jobs.description: One-shot workloads deployed as Kubernetes Jobs. Each job gets a CiliumNetworkPolicy. Jobs run with restartPolicy: OnFailure and a 3-day active deadline. Can be used as ArgoCD resource hooks for lifecycle automation.

name.description: Unique name for the job. Used as the Job name, pod label (platform.lwsa.tech/job), and in network policy selectors.

plan.description: Resource plan that determines CPU and memory requests/limits for the container.

hook.description: ArgoCD resource hook type. Controls when the job runs relative to the sync lifecycle: PreSync (before sync), Sync (during), PostSync (after), SyncFail (on failure), PostDelete (on app deletion), Skip (never run by ArgoCD). When set, the job auto-deletes after 3 days. When omitted, the job persists indefinitely to avoid OutOfSync detection.

command.description: Overrides the container entrypoint (ENTRYPOINT). Each element is a separate argument in the exec form.

args.description: Arguments passed to the container entrypoint (CMD). Used alongside or instead of command.

mountVolumes.description: Mounts persistent volumes (defined in the top-level 'volumes' array) into the container.

name.description: Name of the volume to mount. Must match a volume defined in the top-level 'volumes' array.

path.description: Absolute path inside the container where the volume will be mounted.

ownership.description: Sets ownership of the mount point via an init container that runs chown before the main container starts. Format: 'user:group' (numeric or named, e.g., '1000:1000').

mountConfigMaps.description: Mounts ConfigMaps (defined in the top-level 'configmaps' array) as files inside the container.

name.description: Name of the ConfigMap to mount. Must match a ConfigMap defined in the top-level 'configmaps' array.

path.description: Absolute path inside the container where the ConfigMap will be mounted as a directory of files.

envVars.description: Static environment variables injected into the container. Each key becomes an env var name, each value becomes the env var value.

envVarsFrom.description: Injects environment variables from external sources: Kubernetes Secrets, ConfigMaps, database servers, search engines, or message brokers.

secrets.description: Injects env vars from Kubernetes Secrets. By default all keys from the Secret are injected; use mappings to select specific keys or rename them.

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret to source env vars from.

mappings.description: Selective key injection. Keys are the env var names to set, values are the Secret keys to read from. If omitted, all Secret keys are injected as env vars.

configMaps.description: Injects env vars from Kubernetes ConfigMaps. By default all keys are injected; use mappings to select specific keys or rename them.

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes ConfigMap to source env vars from.

mappings.description: Selective key injection. Keys are the env var names to set, values are the ConfigMap keys to read from. If omitted, all ConfigMap keys are injected as env vars.

dbServers.description: Auto-injects database connection env vars from a database server defined in the top-level 'dbServers' array. The injected vars depend on the database type.

name.description: Name of the database server (must match a dbServers[].name entry).

type.description: Database type. Must match the type of the referenced dbServer.

mappings.description: Override auto-generated env var names. Keys are the custom env var names to set, values are the default env var names to map from.

searches.description: Auto-injects Elasticsearch connection env vars: ELASTICSEARCH_HOST, ELASTICSEARCH_PORT, ELASTICSEARCH_USER, ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD, ELASTICSEARCH_URL.

name.description: Name of the search engine (must match a searches[].name entry).

type.description: Search engine type. Currently only 'elasticsearch' is supported.

messageBrokers.description: Auto-injects message broker connection env vars. For RabbitMQ: RABBITMQ_HOST, RABBITMQ_PORT, RABBITMQ_USER, RABBITMQ_PASSWORD, RABBITMQ_URL.

name.description: Name of the message broker (must match a messageBrokers[].name entry).

type.description: Broker type. Currently only 'rabbitmq' is supported.

egress.description: Controls outbound traffic via CiliumNetworkPolicy egress rules. DNS, S3, MinIO, and intra-namespace traffic are always allowed regardless of these settings.

enabled.description: When false, blocks all egress traffic except DNS, S3, MinIO, and intra-namespace. Defaults to true (all egress allowed).

toCIDRs.description: IP ranges this job is allowed to reach.

port.description: Optional port restriction. If omitted, all ports to this CIDR are allowed.

protocol.description: Protocol for the port restriction. Defaults to TCP if port is specified.

toFQDNs.description: Domain names this job is allowed to reach.

pattern.description: Wildcard domain pattern to allow (e.g., '*.example.com'). Mutually exclusive with 'host'.

host.description: Exact domain name to allow (e.g., 'api.example.com'). Mutually exclusive with 'pattern'.

port.description: Optional port restriction. If omitted, all ports to this FQDN are allowed.

protocol.description: Protocol for the port restriction. Defaults to TCP if port is specified.

dbServers.description: Database server instances managed by Kubernetes operators. Supports MySQL (pxc), PostgreSQL (cnpg), Redis-compatible in-memory (dragonfly), MongoDB-compatible via PostgreSQL backend (ferretdb), and Percona MongoDB (psmdb). Each type creates operator-specific CRDs, auto-generated credentials, and backup configurations.

name.description: Unique name for the database server. Determines the Kubernetes service name used for connections: pxc creates '<name>-pxc', cnpg creates '<name>-rw' (write) and '<name>-r' (read), dragonfly creates '<name>', psmdb creates '<name>-mongos', ferretdb creates '<name>-ferretdb'.

type.description: Database engine type. pxc: Percona XtraDB Cluster (MySQL). cnpg: CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL). dragonfly: Dragonfly (Redis-compatible in-memory store). psmdb: Percona Server for MongoDB. ferretdb: FerretDB (MongoDB-compatible API backed by a CNPG PostgreSQL cluster).

version.description: Database engine version. pxc: '5.7' or '8.0'. cnpg: PostgreSQL major or major.minor version (e.g., '16', '15.3'). ferretdb: PostgreSQL version for the underlying CNPG backend. psmdb: '7.0' or '8.0'. Not used by dragonfly.

plan.description: Resource plan that determines CPU and memory requests/limits for database pods.

size.description: Persistent volume storage size for database data (e.g., '20Gi', '100Gi'). Required for pxc, cnpg, ferretdb, and psmdb. Not used by dragonfly (in-memory only; use snapshot.size for optional persistence).

replicas.description: Number of database replicas. Defaults to 1. For cnpg: must be exactly 1, 3, or 5; replicas >1 enable synchronous replication. For pxc/psmdb: multi-replica setups use pod anti-affinity to spread across nodes.

pause.description: When true, hibernates the database cluster (stops all pods) without deleting persistent data. Available for pxc, cnpg, psmdb, and ferretdb. Set to false or remove to resume.

expose.description: Exposes the database via a LoadBalancer Service, making it reachable from outside the cluster. Use sourceRanges to restrict access.

enabled.description: When true, creates a LoadBalancer Service for external database access.

sourceRanges.description: CIDR ranges allowed to reach the database through the LoadBalancer. If omitted, all source IPs are allowed.

restoreFrom.description: Bootstraps the database by restoring from a backup of another database instance. The source database must have existing backups. Not available during global recovery mode.

name.description: Name of the source database whose backup will be used to restore this instance. Must be a database of the same type that has existing backups.

memcached.description: Enables the memcached protocol interface on Dragonfly (port 11211). Dragonfly only.

enabled.description: When true, enables the memcached protocol on port 11211 in addition to the Redis protocol on port 6379.

snapshot.description: Enables periodic data snapshots to persistent storage for Dragonfly. Snapshots run every 5 minutes. Dragonfly only.

enabled.description: When true, enables periodic snapshots to a PersistentVolumeClaim, allowing data survival across pod restarts.

size.description: PVC storage size for snapshot data (e.g., '10Gi'). Required when snapshot is enabled.

passwordAuth.description: Enables password authentication for Dragonfly. When enabled, a random password is auto-generated and stored in a Kubernetes Secret. Dragonfly only.

enabled.description: When true, requires password authentication for Redis protocol connections. The password is auto-generated and injected as REDIS_PASSWORD into referencing services.

extensions.description: PostgreSQL extensions to enable in the CNPG cluster (e.g., 'pgvector', 'pg_trgm', 'hstore'). cnpg only.

postgresqlConf.description: Custom PostgreSQL configuration parameters passed to the CNPG cluster (e.g., {'shared_buffers': '256MB', 'max_connections': '200'}). cnpg only.

searches.description: Search engine instances managed by the Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) operator. Creates an Elasticsearch cluster with separate master and data node pools, auto-generated credentials, and optional Kibana dashboard.

name.description: Unique name for the Elasticsearch cluster. The HTTP service endpoint will be '<name>-es-http' on port 9200.

type.description: Search engine type. Currently only 'elasticsearch' is supported.

version.description: Elasticsearch version. Can be major only ('8'), major.minor ('8.15'), or major.minor.patch ('8.15.2').

expose.description: Exposes the Elasticsearch HTTP endpoint via a LoadBalancer Service for external access.

enabled.description: When true, creates a LoadBalancer Service for the Elasticsearch HTTP port (9200).

sourceRanges.description: CIDR ranges allowed to reach Elasticsearch through the LoadBalancer. If omitted, all source IPs are allowed.

nodes.description: Node pool configuration. Elasticsearch is deployed with separate master and data node pools. Master nodes handle cluster coordination; data nodes handle indexing, search, and storage.

master.description: Master node pool. Handles cluster state, shard allocation, and coordination. Master nodes are deployed with required anti-affinity across hosts.

replicas.description: Number of master nodes. Defaults to 3. Should be an odd number (1, 3, 5) for proper quorum.

plan.description: Resource plan that determines CPU and memory for master nodes.

size.description: Persistent volume storage size for master node data (e.g., '10Gi').

data.description: Data node pool. Handles indexing, search queries, and data storage. Data nodes are assigned all non-master roles (data, ingest, remote_cluster_client, ml, transform).

replicas.description: Number of data nodes. Defaults to 3.

plan.description: Resource plan that determines CPU and memory for data nodes.

size.description: Persistent volume storage size for data node indices (e.g., '100Gi').

dashboard.description: Deploys a Kibana instance connected to this Elasticsearch cluster for data visualization and management.

enabled.description: When true, deploys a Kibana instance connected to this Elasticsearch cluster.

sourceRanges.description: CIDR ranges allowed to access the Kibana dashboard. If omitted, all source IPs are allowed.

replicas.description: Number of Kibana pod replicas. Defaults to 1.

plan.description: Resource plan that determines CPU and memory for the Kibana pod.

volumes.description: Persistent Volume Claims (PVCs) with ReadWriteOnce (RWO) access mode. Volumes are automatically included in k8up backup schedules. Mount them into services, cronjobs, or jobs via mountVolumes.

name.description: Unique name for the PVC. Referenced by mountVolumes[].name in services, cronjobs, and jobs.

restoreFrom.description: Restores volume data from a restic backup at PVC creation time. Creates a restore Job that pulls the latest snapshot from S3-compatible storage.

name.description: Name of the source volume whose backup will be restored. Must have existing restic snapshots in the backup bucket.

configmaps.description: Kubernetes ConfigMaps for storing non-sensitive configuration data. Can be mounted as files (via mountConfigMaps) or injected as environment variables (via envVarsFrom.configMaps).

name.description: Unique name for the ConfigMap. Referenced by mountConfigMaps[].name and envVarsFrom.configMaps[].name.

data.description: Key-value pairs for the ConfigMap. Each key becomes a file name (when mounted) or env var name (when injected). Values are the file contents or env var values.

secrets.description: Bitnami SealedSecrets for storing sensitive data. Values must be pre-encrypted with the sealed-secrets controller's public key before adding to the blueprint. The controller decrypts them into regular Kubernetes Secrets at deploy time.

name.description: Name of the Secret (after the SealedSecret is decrypted by the controller). Referenced by envVarsFrom.secrets[].name.

namespace.description: Target namespace for the Secret. Must match the namespace where the blueprint is deployed; a validation error occurs on mismatch.

data.description: Encrypted key-value pairs. Each key becomes a Secret data key, each value must be the base64-encoded ciphertext produced by kubeseal.

messageBrokers.description: Message broker instances managed by Kubernetes operators. Creates a RabbitMQ cluster with auto-generated credentials, persistent storage, pod anti-affinity, and a PodDisruptionBudget.

name.description: Unique name for the message broker. The Kubernetes service will be '<name>-rabbitmq'. Credentials are auto-generated in a Secret named '<name>-rabbitmq-default-user'.

type.description: Broker type. Currently only 'rabbitmq' is supported.

plan.description: Resource plan that determines CPU and memory requests/limits for broker pods.

size.description: Persistent volume storage size for message persistence (e.g., '10Gi').

replicas.description: Number of RabbitMQ cluster nodes. Defaults to 3. Uses pod anti-affinity to spread across hosts and pause_minority partition handling.

expose.description: Exposes the RabbitMQ AMQP port via a LoadBalancer Service for external access.

enabled.description: When true, creates a LoadBalancer Service for external RabbitMQ access.

sourceRanges.description: CIDR ranges allowed to reach the broker through the LoadBalancer. If omitted, all source IPs are allowed.

alerting.description: Configures alert routing for this blueprint via Prometheus AlertmanagerConfig. When enabled, alerts matching this namespace are routed to the specified Slack channel.

enabled.description: When true, creates an AlertmanagerConfig that routes alerts for this namespace to the configured Slack channel. Alerts are grouped by alertname with 5-minute intervals and 1-hour repeat.

channel.description: Slack channel where alerts will be sent (e.g., '#my-app-alerts'). Required when alerting is enabled.

users.description: Per-namespace user access control. Users listed here get Kubernetes RoleBindings in this namespace and ArgoCD AppProject role mappings for this project.

view.description: Read-only access. Kubernetes: built-in 'view' ClusterRole scoped to this namespace. ArgoCD: view and sync applications in this project.

debug.description: Debug access (superset of view). Kubernetes: 'view' plus exec into pods, get secrets, port-forward, and scale deployments/statefulsets. ArgoCD: view, sync, exec, and trigger actions on applications in this project.
